topic: HATE full implementation specification readiness Kano audit

kano_candidates.statement: The specification must forbid HATE from reimplementing QEG, RanD, Shipyard, workflow-cookbook, or manual-bb gate authority.

evidence.summary: Guardrails and full implementation contracts preserve gate ownership and forbid publish or verdict override.

kano_candidates.statement: P0a local-first precheck specification must define artifacts, DQ, summary safety, and quickstart acceptance.

evidence.summary: P0a specifies required inputs, outputs, decision contract, DQ coverage, artifact manifest, summary safety, and quickstart acceptance.

kano_candidates.statement: P0b QEG export specification must define qeg-bundle, evidence-map, diff-risk-test, completeness rules, fixtures, failure behavior, CLI, and responsibility boundary.

evidence.summary: The dense P0b contract closes the previous Kano No-Go by defining export inputs, outputs, node/edge rules, gap handling, fixtures, CLI and Shipyard acceptance.

kano_candidates.statement: P1a trust hardening specification must define AETE, adapter capability, canonical identity, retry aggregation, path resolver, replay/compare/explain/recommend/doctor, fixture tree, and failure taxonomy.

evidence.summary: The dense P1a contract closes the previous Kano No-Go by defining schema, scoring rules, command outputs, fixture tree, failure taxonomy, and Shipyard acceptance.

kano_candidates.statement: P1b specification must define RanD alignment, Shipyard evidence, workflow artifacts, and manual-bb bridge without reimplementing upstream gates.

evidence.summary: The full implementation draft defines P1b artifacts and No-Go triggers while preserving upstream verdicts.

kano_candidates.statement: P2/P3 readiness specification must define product and enterprise artifacts while keeping them separate from P0/P1 local-first gates.

evidence.summary: P2/P3 readiness is described as derived reports and PRG metrics, not a dependency for P0/P1 evidence eligibility.

kano_candidates.statement: The specification must separate full implementation spec readiness from implementation completion and preserve No-Go completion gates.

evidence.summary: Readiness criteria define how RanD evaluates spec completeness without converting it into implementation completion claim.

known_biases: audit scope may miss undocumented requirements
implementation evidence can lag behind actual code state
